text.title: Focus Groups Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the focus-groups methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: Each segment MUST be sampled with >=3 groups before themes are treated as stable; single-group themes MUST NOT ship as recommendations.
rule.rationale: Theme stability requires cross-group convergence; single-group themes reflect group-specific dynamics.

rule.statement: Group size MUST be 6-10 participants (recruit 7-11 to absorb 20% no-show); groups outside this range MUST be flagged.
rule.rationale: <6 lacks dynamic; >10 lets quieter participants disappear.

rule.statement: Each topic MUST start with a 60-second written exercise (individual opinion) before open discussion; opinion-anchoring on the loudest voice is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without written-first, dominant participants anchor the group; written-first preserves independent signal.

rule.statement: Concept reveal MUST happen in the last 25% of the session, after vocabulary + needs are mapped; early reveal contaminates language data.
rule.rationale: Early reveal causes participants to use the concept's vocabulary instead of their own.

rule.statement: Moderator MUST be a trained facilitator distinct from the product team author; author MAY observe but not moderate.
rule.rationale: Author bias leaks through leading questions; trained moderators stay neutral.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
